summary: Iteration 2 regression + new features verified. Backend: 23/23 pytest pass including 3 new availability/conflict tests (empty date → blocked_times=[], create 12:00 → blocks 11:30/12:00/13:00 (|diff|<=60 = 409), 14:00 OK; cancelled booking does NOT block and slot is reusable; conflict_window_minutes=60 returned). Frontend: logo renders real image asset (not HTML fallback), gallery section with 4 parallax photos present, nav GALERÍA button works via scrollTo (data-testid='nav-gallery'), schedule shows 'Sábados y Domingos 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM' + 'Lunes a Viernes 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM', map link points to https://maps.google.com/?q=Gotcha%20Los%20Patos%2052743%20La%20Marquesa, footer social links open correct Facebook/Instagram/TikTok URLs, address 'Gotcha Los Patos, 52743 La Marquesa, Méx.' visible. BookingModal time-slot grid: before date → hint 'Selecciona primero una fecha', weekday 2026-06-15 → 4 slots [12,13,14,15], weekend 2026-06-20 → 8 slots [10-17]. After seeding a 12:00 booking, reopening modal on same date renders 12:00 and 13:00 as disabled+line-through (11:00 isn't a weekday slot for 2026-07-10 Friday so could not be verified on that date, but conflict logic from backend validated). 14:00/15:00 remain enabled as expected.

ui_bugs.issue: Previously reported React duplicate-key warning still emits many times when modal opens/date changes (Warning: Encountered two children with the same key). Non-fatal but noisy in console.
Still uses native HTML5 <input type=date> (browser picker) instead of shadcn Calendar/Popover. Already flagged in iteration 1, unchanged.

action_items: Fix duplicate React key warning inside BookingModal (still present from iter 1)
(Optional polish) Replace native <input type=date> with shadcn Calendar+Popover to match dark tactical theme and localize format
critical_code_review_comments: Conflict window logic is symmetric |diff|<=60 so 'exactly 60 min away' blocks (e.g. 12:00 existing → 13:00 rejected). This matches the spec. Verified via pytest.
GET /api/availability is public with no rate limiting — acceptable given read-only, but bots could poll; consider simple throttling if abused.
BookingModal fetches availability on every date change but does not refetch after a successful booking for the same date (not a bug in current flow since modal closes, but worth noting).

Still from iter 1: server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event startup, no login rate-limit/brute-force lockout, CORS allow_origin_regex='.*' + allow_credentials=True is browser-reject combo, secure=False cookie, .to_list(1000) no pagination.
seed_data_creation: Test bookings prefixed TEST_ created during pytest runs and Playwright conflict test; all cleaned up (pytest test_zzz_cleanup + explicit DELETE on the 2026-07-10 12:00 seed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 green. New endpoints/behaviour verified: GET /api/availability (returns {date, blocked_times, conflict_window_minutes:60}), POST /api/bookings returns 409 when |new_t - existing_t| <= 60 min on same date for non-cancelled bookings, cancelled bookings are ignored by the conflict check and do not appear in blocked_times. Frontend time-slot grid with data-testid='time-slot-{HH:MM}' and data-testid='time-slots-grid' working. Gallery section id='gallery' with data-testid='gallery-section' and 4 children data-testid='gallery-photo-0..3'. Navbar uses
rca of the issue: N/A — no regressions or failing features detected in iteration 2.
